_sources.discrepancies: Hawaii is a universal vote-by-mail state since 2020 (Act 136). NCSL classifies Hawaii as photo ID requested but not strictly required — signature verification on mail ballots is the primary security mechanism. The Hawaii primary is typically the second Saturday in August. TODO verify exact 2026 primary date; estimated 2026-08-08. Conditional same-day registration is available only for voters who missed the regular deadline due to a qualifying reason.

earlyVoting.notes: All registered voters receive a mail ballot. In-person voter service centers (VSCs) open for in-person voting and assistance. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.
All registered voters receive a mail ballot automatically approximately 18 days before the election. Voter service centers open for in-person voting. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.

Hawaii is a universal vote-by-mail state. All registered voters automatically receive a mail ballot. Voter service centers open for in-person voting and ballot drop-off. No traditional polling places — vote from home or at a voter service center.

acceptedIds: Hawaii driver's license or state ID
Employee ID with photo and signature
Student ID from Hawaii school or university
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Hawaii voter service centers allow use of phones and electronic devices to assist with voting. Photography of other voters or completed ballots for public posting requires consent. Hawaii is a vote-by-mail state — most voters vote at home.
additionalRules: Hawaii is a universal vote-by-mail state — all registered voters automatically receive a mail ballot; no application needed.
Hawaii has an open primary — all voters regardless of party registration may vote in any party's primary. There is no party enrollment system.
Voter service centers are open for in-person voting if you prefer not to mail your ballot.
Photo ID may be requested but is not strictly required — signature on ballot envelope is the primary verification mechanism.

eligibility: All registered voters automatically receive a ballot by mail
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: No application required — ballots are automatically mailed to all registered voters
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by 7:00 p.m. on Election Day, or postmarked by Election Day and received within 10 days
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Drop off by 7 p.m. on Election Day or mail postmarked by Election Day (received within 10 days).

primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEn: Hawaii has an open primary (HRS §11-601). Any registered voter may participate in any party's primary. There is no requirement to pre-register with a party.
primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEs: Hawái tiene una primaria abierta (HRS §11-601). Cualquier votante registrado puede participar en la primaria de cualquier partido. No se requiere registro previo con un partido.
